title: Da'Gara
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Yuuzhan Vong |image=[[File:DaGaraInfobox-NJOS.png]] |name=Da'Gara |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], [[Helska IV]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'', which include Da'Gara's [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Helska IV]], to [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. |species=[[Yuuzhan Vong]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Praetorite Vong]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I—Da'Gara. Prefect and adviser to yammosk, war coordinator oo-oo-oof Praetorite Vong. Welcome my home. You see my home, I be-bel-believe. You come me there. I show you splendor Yuuzhan Vong.|Prefect Da'Gara introduces himself to Danni Quee's scientists through a villip|}} [[Prefect (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Prefect]] '''Da'Gara''' was a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was a key member of the [[Praetorite Vong]], the advance force that established a foothold in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During its [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]], he commanded a ''[[Koros-Strohna]]'' [[Da'Gara's worldship|worldship]], which had journeyed to the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. The Yuuzhan Vong [[Operative|agent]] [[Yomin Carr]] ensure that no one witnessed the ''Koros-Strohna''{{'s}} arrival at the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Helska IV]] by incapacitating the [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] at the nearby planet of [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]], allowing Da'Gara to construct a [[Helska IV base|base]] on Helska in secret. However, [[Danni Quee]] and other scientists, escaped and found the base. As the Yuuzhan Vong prefect's forces moved to attack the scientist's [[Spacecaster-class shuttle|shuttle]], Quee attempted to escape, forcing the Yuuzhan Vong to shoot down her vessel, ultimately crashing on Helska. As Carr deemed her worthy, Da'Gara did not kill Quee and treated her with respect and did not [[Death/Legends|kill]] her dishonorably, instead seeking to sacrifice her to the Praetorite Vong's [[Unidentified yammosk|yammosk]], a creature that telepathically coordinated Da'Gara's forces. After [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] [[Skirmish at Helska IV|engaged]] Yuuzhan Vong ''[[yorik-et]]'' starfighters over the prefect's base, ultimately being destroyed or forced to flee, Da'Gara [[Mission to Sernpidal|destroyed]] the nearby planet of [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]] by [[Yo'gand's Core|pulling]] [[Dobido|one]] of its [[Moon/Legends|moons]] into it, as he believed that Sernpidal was the largest threat to his forces. The planet was utterly destroyed, and while the [[yammosk]] dispatched four battle groups to pursue a [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]] convoy that had fled Sernpidal's destruction, a New Republic [[Task force/Legends|task force]] [[Battle of Helska IV|attacked]] the prefect's base. The yammosk and the prefect joined their minds and led their forces, with the former using its telepathic abilities to coordinate many ''yorik-et'' fighters during the defense of the Yuuzhan Vong base. However, the New Republic's [[shieldship]]s used the [[Helska system/Legends|Helska system's]] [[Helska system's star|sun's]] energy to evaporate Helska IV's [[Water/Legends|icy]] surface by directing the [[Star/Legends|star's]] energy at the planet, killing Da'Gara and all of his warriors. ==Biography== ===Secret plans=== {{Quote|Who helped the Praetorite Vong obtain a yammosk—and an imperfect one at that?''»
«''I may have mentioned something—|Supreme Commander Nas Choka and Executor Nom Anor|''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''|translated=1}} [[File:Shimrra_TUF.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Praetorite Vong and Prefect Da'Gara swore loyalty to Supreme Overlord Shimrra Jamaane (pictured).]] Da'Gara was a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served his [[Species/Legends|species']] [[Yuuzhan Vong empire|empire]]. During his [[Prefect (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|prefect]] training, Da'Gara helped perform several [[breaking]]s, a technique of [[Torture/Legends|torture]] practiced by the Yuuzhan Vong that consisted of incapacitating a victim by degrading them mentally so that they were unable to fight back, ultimately [[Death/Legends|executing]] them. The Yuuzhan Vong [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] became a prefect and a key member''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' of the [[Praetorite Vong]], the Yuuzhan Vong's advance force in their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] the Yuuzhan Vong empire sought to conquer. The Yuuzhan Vong [[executor]] [[Nom Anor]] gave the Praetorite Vong a [[Unidentified yammosk|yammosk]]''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''—a telepathic creature capable of coordinating Yuuzhan Vong forces in battle—without authorization to allow Da'Gara to further his plans for the invasion. In [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' the ruler of the Yuuzhan Vong, [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Quoreal]], was [[Jamaane coup|executed]] by [[Shimrra Jamaane]], who was one of the candidates to succeed Quoreal. While the Praetorite Vong and Prefect Da'Gara swore loyalty to Jamaane, who succeeded Quoreal, their fealty to the Supreme Overlord was a ruse to keep the prefect's invasion plans secret.''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' ===Terraforming and an invasion=== {{Quote|The Mandalorians appear to be best suited for infiltration, retrieval, assassination, and sabotage. In the year I've been using them, they've proved reliable. Their small numbers make them worthless as an army, although they might make an excellent enslaved division at a future date. Goran Beviin did an efficient job of removing B'Leph, and a civil war is still in progress. He recruits equally efficient comrades: even their children are savage fighters. When I spoke to their leader, the one they call Mandalore—Boba Fett—I feared for a while that he might want more answers than I could give him. But the kind of destabilization and execution they excel at is a normal, everyday occurrence within this corrupt galaxy; he has no reason to wonder why I ask what I do of his people. He's seen and fought wars before. Like me, he's a realist. A practical man. I almost look forward to meeting him. Mandalore is already on my list as a world that will be harder to subdue.|Executor Nom Anor, in a report to Prefect Da'Gara|''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''}} [[File:Helska4.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Prefect Da'Gara's forces journeyed to the planet of Helska IV as part of a Yuuzhan Vong invasion of the galaxy.]] In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], while Da'Gara's forces traveled across the galaxies before entering their target galaxy, Anor allied himself with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], a people from the galaxy the Yuuzhan Vong sought to conquer. The executor sent a report on the Mandalorians' capabilities to Da'Gara eight [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] before the Praetorite Vong arrived in the galaxy. As the prefect's ''[[Koros-Strohna]]'' [[Da'Gara's worldship|worldship]] traveled to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Helska IV]], a Yuuzhan Vong warrior and [[Operative|agent]] named [[Yomin Carr]] made sure that no one witnessed the worldship's arrival. To achieve this, he incapacitated most of the [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] stationed at the [[ExGal-4]] station on the nearby Outer Rim Territories planet of [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]]. While Carr ravaged Belkadan, Da'Gara and one of his servants, [[Tu Shoolb]], discussed that the scientists could spot them. Shoolb claimed that accelerating their worldship's speed with its [[dovin basal]]s, [[Biot|living organisms]] that propelled worldships, would alert those watching. The prefect asked if Shoolb believed that Carr had not completed his task, prompting an apology from the other, who accelerated Da'Gara's worldship. The prefect almost reconsidered his command, but decided against it, as he was anxious to arrive at his destination. ===Worthiness and honor=== {{Quote|Choice none. You fol-follow coralskippers in. Now! Or you melt and we take from you honor of gift to Yun-Yammka.|Prefect Da'Gara, to the scientists aboard the Spacecaster shuttle|}} [[File:Quee1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Prefect Da'Gara's forces shot down Danni Quee's shuttle.]] Once he arrived, the Praetorite Vong's [[yammosk]] helped land worldship on Helska IV, using its single tooth to bring the ''Koros-Strohna'' beneath the planet's icy crust and creating an energy field around the world. The prefect donned an [[ooglith cloaker]], a being that protected his body from Helska's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], which did not contain any [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] and would therefore kill Da'Gara, and a [[gnullith]] creature, which allowed the prefect to breathe on the planet. The prefect's warriors then constructed a [[Helska IV base|base]] with the worldship. After Carr incapacitated the scientists on Belkadan, he communicated with Da'Gara through a living communicator called a [[villip]]. The agent reported that he had begun [[terraforming]] Belkadan for the Yuuzhan Vong's purposes and that it would be destroyed and ready after a few [[Standard day/Legends|days]]. He also told Da'Gara about one of the scientists, [[Danni Quee]], who Carr believed was worthy of a Yuuzhan Vong sacrifice. The prefect agreed to honor the agent's request, seeking to sacrifice her to the yammosk instead of killing her dishonorably. Quee and a group of scientists were able to escape, however, using a [[Spacecaster-class shuttle|''Spacecaster''-class shuttle]] to travel to Helska. The Yuuzhan Vong prefect had hundreds of his ''[[yorik-et]]'' [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], also known as coralskippers, intercept the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]]. The coralskippers fired a villip at the Spacecaster, with the living communicator relaying a message from Da'Gara to the scientists. The prefect welcomed them to Helska and invited them to his base; however, they tried to flee, prompting the ''yorik-et'' fighters to attack the shuttle and send it crashing down to the planet below. ===Respect and promises=== {{Quote|Yomin Carr demand respect for Danni Quee. Do you und-under—''"
"''Understand.''"
"''You understand the honor?|Prefect Da'Gara and Danni Quee|}} All the scientists but Quee and [[Bensin Tomri]] surived, and the Yuuzhan Vong prefect killed the latter by driving his hand into the other's throat. Quee was brought to Da'Gara's worldship, where the prefect spoke with her. He asked her to understand that Carr deeming her worthy was a huge honor, and shoved her into the water below the worldship, accompanying her. Shortly afterward, Gara spoke with Anor through villip. The prefect reported that Carr had terraformed Belkadan and that he was ready to use ExGal-4's visual scopes to his people's advantage. Da'Gara and the executor agreed that the largest threat to the discovery of the prefect's forces was the [[Julevian system]]'s planet [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]]. Da'Gara suggested using [[Yo'gand's Core]]—where a [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] manipulating dovin basal was used to crash a planet's [[Moon/Legends|moon]] into itself—to destroy the planet. [[File:NomAnor-TERC.png|thumb|right|300px|Executor Nom Anor advised Prefect Da'Gara.]] After Da'Gara's conversation with Anor, a [[Kyp's Dozen|squadron]] of starfighters of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—the major power in the galaxy—[[Skirmish at Helska IV|engaged]] several of the prefect's coralskippers. The [[Squadron/Legends|squadron's]] members, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] of the New Republic, were shot down, and only two of them were able to escape. One of the starfighters, [[Miko Reglia]]'s, crashed on Helska, and Da'Gara's warriors captured the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Prison/Legends|imprisoning]] him. Da'Gara brought Reglia before Quee, putting a [[tizowyrm]] in his ear to know how to speak in the other's [[Language/Legends|language]], [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. He identified the Jedi as unworthy as part of Reglia's mental breaking. Gara then reported to Anor, telling the executor of the two [[Starship/Legends|starfighters']] escape and claiming that the fighter craft would not get far, as several [[grutchin]]s—insectoid Yuuzhan Vong creatures that tore through starships—were attached to the vessels. He shared his plans to sacrifice Reglia to the yammosk after he was broken and that a second worldship was to join his on Helska that day and that a third one would join within a week. In response, the executor warned him to prepare for a formidable New Republic attack. Reglia and Quee escaped their compartment and arrived at a large chamber where Da'Gara was. Reglia, who had taken an [[amphistaff]] melee weapon from one of his captors, rushed at the prefect, who avoided the attack and began his attack. He threw goo at the Jedi, who was paralyzed by the material. The prefect invited Quee to a small alcove, letting her witness the arrival of the second worldship, commanded by Prefect [[Ma'Shraid]]. The scientist and the Jedi, along with most of Da'Gara and Ma'Shraid's warriors then joined the yammosk. Da'Gara did likewise later. ===Blasphemy and confidence=== {{Quote|''Do-ro'ik vong pratte.''''»
«''''Do-ro'ik vong pratte''. And woe to our enemies.|Executor Nom Anor and Prefect Da'Gara, calling for the release of the Yuuzhan Vong's ferocity||translated=1}} The prefect joined his mind with that of the yammosk, communicating with his troops through the war coordinator. He spoke of the ''zhaetor-shae'' of the Praetorite Vong—the faction's glory—about his forces' victory in their skirmish with the New Republic, and Shraid, whose forces then joined the gathering. The prefects gave several speeches, and Reglia was carried before the yammosk, who devoured the Jedi. After the sacrifice, the warriors left the gathering, and Da'Gara once again joined with the yammosk before speaking with Anor. [[File:Inv07-Yammosk.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Prefect Da'Gara joined his mind with that of the yammosk (one such being pictured) during the battle of Helska IV.]] He reported that Sernpidal had been [[Destruction of Sernpidal|utterly destroyed]] and that the yammosk had dispatched four battle groups to follow a [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]] convoy fleeing the planet to the next world before the pair called for the release of the Yuuzhan Vong's ferocity in their [[Yuuzhan Vong (language)|language]]. After the convoy led the four battle groups to the planet of [[Dubrillion/Legends|Dubrillion]] and the subsequent [[Battle of Dubrillion|engagement]] that ended with the Yuuzhan Vong's defeat, Da'Gara, Shraid, and the commander of the third worldship, Prefect [[Dooje Brolo]], discussed their plans. They agreed that after the New Republic attacked their forces at Helska, they would conquer Dubrillion and its sister planet, [[Destrillion]], and turn one of them into their second base. The New Republic launched an [[Battle of Helska IV|attack]] on Helska shortly afterward, attacking with a large force led by the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Rejuvenator]]''. The Jedi [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]] and [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] used the [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] vessel ''[[Merry Miner]]'' and an [[iceborer]] vessel, used to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] ice planets, to open Helska's crust to let the New Republic forces attack Da'Gara's base. The prefect's coralskippers detected the Jedi and attacked them, with Jacen going to the world's surface to rescue Quee. While Da'Gara's warriors fought Jacen and killed Reglia, the ''Rejuvenator'' and its complement arrived. The yammosk sent a small group of coralskippers to confront the New Republic forces and bring them closer to a much larger force of Yuuzhan Vong starfighters. The war coordinator coordinated the Yuuzhan Vong forces in the battle, during which the enemy forces retreated. They returned with a smaller task force, and Da'Gara rushed to join the yammosk. As the force included [[shieldship]]s, capable of shielding ships from a [[Star/Legends|star]], the prefect was worried that his enemies would try to destroy the war coordinator's field protecting the planet and the dovin basals on the world; however, as the war coordinator was confident that they would easily repel the enemy force, Da'Gara calmed himself, hoping that he would be able keep Quee—who had escaped—after the battle. The New Republic shieldships, however, used the [[Helska system's star|Helskan sun's]] energy to evaporate Helska's ice. As Da'Gara's coralskippers destroyed enemy forces, the icy planet was shattered. All on the surface, including Da'Gara, were killed. Anor later tried to contact the late prefect and Carr, but failed to do so. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|Prefect Da'Gara and the Praetorite Domains—what did their blasphemous actions earn them but ice graves on what little remains of Helska 4, so far removed from Yuuzhan'tar it might as well be in the galaxy we left behind?|Supreme Overlord Shimrra Jamaane|}} Da'Gara's defeat—which Yuuzhan Vong [[Supreme Commander (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Supreme Commander]] [[Nas Choka]] deemed a miscalculation—disgraced Anor,''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' as the executor had helped the prefect. Da'Gara's actions at Helska were deemed blasphemous by Shimrra, who believed that they had only earnt the prefect and his warriors ice graves on Helska's remains. Ultimately, the Yuuzhan Vong's invasion ended with their [[Sekot accords|surrender]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|There are ways for you to join with us. You will learn. You will learn the glory of the Praetorite Vong. You will learn your place.|Prefect Da'Gara, to Danni Quee|}} [[File:Dagara.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Da'Gara, prefect of the Praetorite Vong]] A strong and powerful male Yuuzhan Vong warrior and [[Officer/Legends|officer]], Prefect Da'Gara was astute and resourceful. The example of what a member of the Yuuzhan Vong [[warrior caste]] should be, the prefect was ruthless and fanatical, being an independent thinker with brutal efficiency while obeying the Yuuzhan Vong [[warmaster]], the leader of the Yuuzhan Vong's military forces. He almost reconsidered his order to Tu Shoolb, but his eagerness to begin his operations on Helska prompted him not to. Once he heard that Carr considered Quee worthy, he considered the possibility of [[Bioengineering/Legends|converting]] the scientist to a Yuuzhan Vong warrior; however, Carr believed that task would be impossible since Quee would resist the process, and Da'Gara accepted to perform a worthy sacrifice to the yammosk. The prefect did not want the agent to die on Belkadan, as Carr had performed well on Belkadan. An ambitious Yuuzhan Vong, Prefect Da'Gara sought to lead the Praetorite Vong's conquest of the galaxy before the main [[Yuuzhan Vong fleet|invasion force]] arrived. When confronting Quee's scientists through a villip, he instructed them to follow his coralskippers to Helska or die and have their "honor of gift" from [[Yun-Yammka]]—the Yuuzhan Vong [[Deity/Legends|god]] of war—taken away from them. The prefect told Quee that she would learn the glory of the Praetorite Vog, promising that whether or not she was converted or killed, that he would make Yun-Yammka happier. Contrary to Anor's opinion, Da'Gara claimed that the New Republic starfighters' escape would not be a problem, assuring the executor that they would not make it far. He regretted that the region of the galaxy surrounding Helska IV was sparsely populated, as that did not please him. Da'Gara repeatedly referred to Reglia as unworthy and used Quee against the Jedi as part of his breaking. The prefect had a bald, disfigured, and tattooed head, a broken nose, and [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|You've learned our language.''"
"''We have our ways, Danni Quee. You will learn.|Prefect Da'Gara and Danni Quee, on the former's capability to speak Basic|}} Da'Gara was skilled in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|melee combat]], and combat with [[tsaisi]]s, Yuuzhan Vong [[Baton/Legends|batons]]. He was a native speaker of the Yuuzhan Vong language and able to speak Basic with a tizowyrm; while Quee deemed the prefect's Basic sentence structure horrible and the prefect stuttered over every word, he was eventually able to speak smoothly in the language, surprising Quee. ==Equipment== The prefect owned a [[coufee]] dagger,[[Invasion 3|''Invasion'' 3]] several villip communicators, and a worldship. He wore [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric|vonduun crab armor]] and wielded an amphistaff. ==Behind the scenes== Prefect Da'Gara appeared in the [[1999]] novel [[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime|''Vector Prime'']], written by [[R. A. Salvatore]] as the first volume in the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' novel series. [[James Luceno]]'s [[2000]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'' misspells Da'Gara as "Da'gara."''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'' The [[2002]] sourcebook ''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', establishes that Da'Gara was the [[Commanding officer/Legends|leader]] of the Praetorite Vong; ''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'', another Wizards of the Coast sourcebook for use with the ''Star Wars Roleplaying Game'', states that a [[Unidentified high prefect|high prefect]] led the advance force. However, the latter sourcebook also states that Da'Gara was the leader of the Praetorite Vong. This articles assumes that Da'Gara was not the Praetorite Vong's leader. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Da'Gara}} *''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' {{1st}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Da'Gara |de=Da'gara |es=Da'Gara |hu=Da'Gara }} [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Officers]] [[Category:Yuuzhan Vong warriors]]
